bonuses.leadin: Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport was modeled on this building, which also used to use mobile lounges to transport its users. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building, which is entirely open inside because of its notable catenary curve roof. That roof is supported by walls that lean outward and which have columns that go through the roof.

This other airport terminal by Eero Saarinen has a wing-shaped roof divided into four curved shells and glass walls that come to two opposing points. Like Dulles, it has an open interior, but with a central balcony within the terminal.
Saarinen also used a thin-shell section of a sphere to roof his Kresge Auditorium for this college campus. He also designed a cylindrical chapel with undulating brick walls of this campus.
answers: Washington Dulles International Airport, Main Terminal
Trans World Airlines Flight Center [prompt on “John F. Kennedy International Airport”, or on “JFK Terminal 5” ]
answers_sanitized: Washington Dulles International Airport, Main Terminal
Trans World Airlines Flight Center [prompt on "John F. Kennedy International Airport", or on "JFK Terminal 5" ]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport was modeled on this building, which also used to use mobile lounges to transport its users. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this building, which is entirely open inside because of its notable catenary curve roof. That roof is supported by walls that lean outward and which have columns that go through the roof.

This other airport terminal by Eero Saarinen has a wing-shaped roof divided into four curved shells and glass walls that come to two opposing points. Like Dulles, it has an open interior, but with a central balcony within the terminal.
Saarinen also used a thin-shell section of a sphere to roof his Kresge Auditorium for this college campus. He also designed a cylindrical chapel with undulating brick walls of this campus.

bonuses.leadin: This architect’s flexibility in creating the design of the General Motors Technical Center in Warren, Michigan earned him commissions for several other corporate headquarters. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this architect who also designed headquarters for John Deere, IBM, and CBS.
Saarinen created an airfoil-like concrete roof in his design for this Washington, D.C.-area airport.
Eero Saarinen furnished the interiors of his corporate headquarters buildings with his own “Grasshopper,” “Womb,” and “Tulip” chairs from this furniture company.
